discovery.run_summary: Risk-on tape with semis leading (NVDA +4.4%, AVGO +5.5%, TSM +4.5%, SMH +1%) on AI/China-trade optimism around the Trump-Xi summit. VIX down to 17.3. Mid-cap movers list is light on visible catalysts — none of the six flagged movers have 8-K filings attached, making most of these UNCLEAR or sympathy-driven reads.

notable_index_moves: SMH +1.03% led by NVDA/AVGO/TSM on AI demand + China trade thaw
XLB -0.75% and XLRE -0.68% the only red sectors — commercial real estate weak (JLL move fits)
VIX -3.4% to 17.27, supportive of risk appetite
Cerebras IPO popped 68% — fresh AI-pureplay supply

discoveries.name: Jones Lang LaSalle Incorporated
discoveries.setup: Mid-cap CRE services name down 7% on below-average volume with no 8-K and no visible single-day catalyst, extending a -12% 5-day slide
discoveries.thesis: Move is the continuation of a multi-day decline in a weak sub-sector (XLRE -0.7% on a risk-on tape), but a 7% single-day drop on 0.9x volume with no filing is unusual — either a delayed reaction to the prior earnings print, an analyst action, or news not yet in our feed. I can't articulate either direction at conf 3+ without identifying the trigger.
discoveries.what_confirms: Find a downgrade, guidance cut, or commercial real estate transaction-volume data point dated today; or a recent 8-K we missed
discoveries.what_kills: If no specific catalyst surfaces and the stock stabilizes tomorrow, then this was just illiquid continuation selling and the gap fades
discoveries.what_to_learn: Single-day -7% on sub-1x volume usually means the marginal seller is motivated/forced rather than reacting to fresh news — worth a same-day check for index rebalance, secondary, or large block
discoveries.catalyst: No identified catalyst; XLRE weak but magnitude exceeds sector move materially
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: catalyst_signals empty; XLRE -0.68% does not explain a -7% single-name move
research_pointers: Check for analyst downgrades or price target cuts today on JLL or peers (CBRE, CWK)
Look at recent commercial real estate transaction data / leasing volume releases
Review the 5-day chart — was there a recent earnings print that's still being digested?
Check option flow for unusual put activity preceding today's move

discoveries.name: Saia, Inc.
discoveries.setup: LTL trucker up 6%+ on roughly average volume with no 8-K — possible sympathy with broader cyclical/trade-thaw narrative but magnitude high for that alone
discoveries.thesis: Trump-Xi summit + Boeing/China order headlines are bullish for goods-trade-exposed industrials, and LTL is a leading freight indicator. But XLI is only +0.5% and SAIA's move is 13x the sector — either a peer (ODFL, XPO) reported something positive, a freight-volume data print landed, or there's a SAIA-specific item not in our feed. Cannot commit to a direction without identifying it.
discoveries.what_confirms: A peer LTL print or Cass Freight Index release dated today, or an analyst upgrade tied to freight recovery
discoveries.what_kills: If no peer/industry catalyst is identifiable, the move is likely a squeeze on the trade-thaw narrative and reverses if China headlines disappoint
discoveries.catalyst: Possibly Trump-Xi trade optimism + cyclicals reflation; no SAIA-specific filing
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: catalyst_signals empty; sector move (+0.5%) far smaller than stock move
research_pointers: Check whether ODFL, XPO, ARCB moved similarly today (peer confirmation of LTL bid)
Look for Cass Freight Index or ATA tonnage data released today
Check SAIA short interest — high-SI freight names often squeeze on China-trade days

discoveries.name: Extreme Networks
discoveries.setup: Networking equipment small-mid-cap up 8.5% on modest volume in a strong XLK/SMH tape, no 8-K
discoveries.thesis: Plausibly riding AI-networking sympathy (AVGO +5.5%, ANET-adjacent demand narrative), but +8.5% on 1.37x volume with no filing typically indicates either an upgrade, a contract win not yet in our feed, or speculative pre-positioning. Direction unclear without identifying the trigger.
discoveries.what_confirms: Analyst upgrade, contract/design-win press release, or peer (ANET, JNPR, CSCO) commentary about networking demand
discoveries.what_kills: If trigger turns out to be a rumor or speculative AI-tag, fade risk is high once the broader semis rally cools
discoveries.catalyst: Possibly AI-networking sympathy with AVGO; no company-specific filing
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: catalyst_signals empty; XLK +1.5% does not justify 8.5% single-name
research_pointers: Search for EXTR analyst actions, partner/customer announcements today
Check ANET and JNPR moves for sector-wide networking bid
Review EXTR short interest and options activity

discoveries.name: Beam Therapeutics
discoveries.setup: Pre-revenue biotech down 7% on roughly average volume in a weak XBI tape (-0.74%); no filing
discoveries.thesis: Gene-editing biotechs are high-beta to XBI and risk-off in healthcare specifically (XLV flat, XBI down). BEAM and CRSP both down meaningfully — likely sector-driven de-risking within gene editing rather than company-specific bad news. Magnitude is large but biotechs of this profile routinely move 7% on no news.
discoveries.what_confirms: If CRSP, NTLA, VERV, EDIT all show similar weakness, confirms sector rotation read
discoveries.what_kills: If a Beam-specific pipeline update, partnership unwinding, or clinical readout headline emerges, the read needs revision
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: catalyst_signals empty; CRSP -4.4% on same day supports sector read
research_pointers: Verify NTLA, VERV, EDIT moves to confirm subgroup weakness
Check for any conference presentations or abstract releases (ASGCT timing)
Watch cash runway commentary — high-burn biotechs sell off harder on risk-off days

discoveries.name: Kratos Defense
discoveries.setup: Defense small/mid-cap up 4.5% while ITA defense ETF down 0.55% — divergence worth noting
discoveries.thesis: KTOS up while ITA down is the interesting cross-current — suggests company-specific bid (drone/unmanned-systems momentum, possible contract), not sector sympathy. Without a filing I can't confirm direction with conviction, but the divergence from the sector ETF is a real signal worth surfacing.
discoveries.what_confirms: DoD contract announcement, congressional defense bill markup language favoring unmanned systems, or competitor commentary
discoveries.what_kills: If no specific catalyst surfaces, the move may be a low-conviction momentum chase that gives back when ITA rebounds
discoveries.what_to_learn: Single-name outperformance vs sector ETF on the same day (+5% vs -0.5%) is a stronger signal than absolute move — flags genuine idiosyncratic bid
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: catalyst_signals empty; ITA -0.55% makes the move idiosyncratic not sympathetic
research_pointers: Check DoD daily contract awards page for KTOS or subsidiaries
Review AVAV, RKLB, and other drone/space names for thematic confirmation
Look at recent KTOS options activity and any sell-side notes

discovery.no_signals_note: Movers list is unusually catalyst-light — zero 8-Ks across six names. Most flags are UNCLEAR-conf-2 by design: the discipline here is to not manufacture theses when the signal isn't there. JLL's -7% on sub-1x volume is the highest-priority follow-up because the magnitude/volume divergence usually implies an identifiable trigger (downgrade, block, or news we haven't ingested).

ai_announcements.headline: Cisco cuts nearly 4,000 jobs to spend more on AI, reports record quarterly revenue
ai_announcements.summary: Cisco announces layoffs to redirect spend toward AI while reporting record revenue.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Extreme Networks is a direct competitor in enterprise networking. Cisco's record revenue read-through plus AI-driven networking demand (AI datacenter buildout requires switching/routing) is a positive tailwind for the whole networking equipment category. Cisco layoffs could also create displacement opportunities for EXTR.
research_pointers: Check if EXTR has reported recent earnings or guidance
Confirm AI datacenter networking demand commentary from Cisco call
Volume multiple 1.37x suggests catalyst-driven, not just sympathy

ai_announcements.headline: Clio hits $500M ARR as Anthropic ups the ante in legal tech
ai_announcements.summary: Legal tech adoption accelerating; Anthropic pushing deeper into legal vertical. ANTHROPIC-RELATED CALL - flagged for bias grading.

ai_announcements.headline: Cerebras IPO raises $5.5B, stock pops 108%
ai_announcements.summary: First major AI hardware IPO of 2026, signaling strong AI infrastructure appetite.

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: Most movers in market_data (BEAM, JLL, SAIA, KTOS, CRSP) have no clear AI-news linkage to today's announcements. JLL down 7% on 0.92x volume is more likely earnings/CRE-related than AI. BEAM/CRSP biotech selloff unrelated. KTOS defense move unrelated. SAIA trucking move unrelated. Only EXTR shows a plausible AI-news mechanism (Cisco read-through).
